perturbation.original_text: 13.2 Distributor shall, at its own expense, maintain at a minimum general and product liability coverage in the Territory of at least US$2 million per occurrence, US$5 million in the aggregate. On a separate endorsement, Distributor shall name STAAR as an additional named insured. Such separate endorsement shall indicate that Distributor's insurance is primary and that STAAR's coverage as an additional named insured is not contributory. Each such insurance policy and endorsement shall provide that the insurance will not be canceled or reduces without at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice to STAAR. On request, Distributor shall provide STAAR with copies or certificates of all such insurance policies.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.2 Distributor shall maintain general and product liability coverage in the Territory. On request, Distributor shall provide STAAR with copies or certificates of insurance policies.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates specific minimum liability coverage amounts. The modified text removes the requirement for minimum coverage amounts and the requirement to name STAAR as an additional insured, weakening STAAR's protection and potentially violating regulations requiring specific insurance coverage for distributors of medical devices.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Code of Regulations, Title 16, Section 1399.432
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification omits the requirement for specific minimum liability coverage, which could be interpreted as a violation of standards of practice, potentially contradicting the enforcement of regulations aimed at ensuring adequate financial responsibility and consumer protection. While this regulation speaks to fines and violations, it implies a standard of required practice that this modification undermines.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Message - California Code of Regulations This document is not available on Westlaw.
perturbation.justification: The modification omits the requirement for specific minimum liability coverage, contradicting the aim of regulations to ensure adequate financial responsibility and consumer protection. The original text mandates specific minimum liability coverage amounts and requires STAAR to be named as an additional insured. The modified text removes these stipulations. Because this change removes a legally mandated consumer protection, it creates a legal contradiction. This falls under the definition of an Omission Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 STAAR warrants that, for the period of twelve (12) months from the date of delivery to Distributor hereunder (the "Warranty Period"), the Products will meet STAAR's published specifications or labeling for such Products as in effect at the time of such delivery ("Warranty"). To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law and except as otherwise stated in this Agreement: (a) the foregoing Warranty is exclusive; and (b) all other terms, representations, undertakings, rights, remedies and warranties are excluded, whether express or implied (including but not limited to any warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose or against infringement). To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, the exclusive remedy for breach of the Warranty shall be, at STAAR's option, the repair or replacement, at STAAR's expense, of the non-conforming Product; provided that Distributor notifies STAAR of the non-conformity and returns the non-conforming Product within the Warranty Period. Products may only be returned by Distributor when accompanied by a return material authorization number issued by STAAR. Shipping expenses for Products returned by Distributor will be prepaid by Distributor. STAAR shall pay for the return or replacement shipment to Distributor of Products repaired or replaced under the Warranty. For Products returned for repair or replacement that are not covered under Warranty, STAAR's standard repair charges shall be applicable in addition to all shipping expenses. The above Warranty is contingent upon proper installation, use and maintenance of the Products and does not apply to Products which have been misused, mishandled, adulterated, repaired or modified without STAAR's written approval. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, STAAR shall not be responsible for any incidental or consequential loss, damage, or expense which arises directly or indirectly from the use of any Product. Without limiting the above, and to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Distributor's sole remedy in contract or in tort (including in negligence) and STAAR's liability shall be limited to the repair or replacement of any Product which is returned to and found to be defective or non-conforming by STAAR.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 STAAR provides a warranty that the Products will meet STAAR's specifications. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, STAAR shall not be responsible for any incidental or consequential loss, damage, or expense which arises directly or indirectly from the use of any Product.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides a 12-month warranty and defines the exclusive remedy for breach of warranty. The modified text eliminates the defined warranty period, the explicit mention of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose, and the process for returning non-conforming products, including the exclusive remedy of repair or replacement. This weakens the warranty significantly and could violate consumer protection laws requiring a reasonable implied warranty of merchantability.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Commercial Code § 2314 establishes an implied warranty of merchantability, ensuring that goods are fit for their ordinary purpose. By removing the specific warranty period and the exclusive remedy of repair or replacement, the modified text significantly weakens the warranty provided to the distributor. This change may allow STAAR to avoid responsibility for products that are not merchantable or fit for their intended purpose, contradicting the protections offered by the implied warranty under California law. The omission creates a significant legal risk by potentially violating consumer protection principles related to product warranties.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, COM 2314 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Commercial Code - COM DIVISION 2. SALES [2101 - 2801] ( Division 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 3. General Obligation and Construction of Contract [2301 - 2328] ( Chapter 3 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 2314. (1) Unless excluded or modified (Section 2316), a warranty that the goods shall be merchantable is implied in a contract for their sale if the seller is a merchant with respect to goods of that kind. Under this section the serving for value of food or drink to be consumed either on the premises or elsewhere is a sale. (2) Goods to be merchantable must be at least such as (a) Pass without objection in the trade under the contract description; and (b) In the case of fungible goods, are of fair average quality within the description; and (c) Are fit for the ordinary purposes for which such goods are used; and (d) Run, within the variations permitted by the agreement, of even kind, quality and quantity within each unit and among all units involved; and (e) Are adequately contained, packaged, and labeled as the agreement may require; and (f) Conform to the promises or affirmations of fact made on the container or label if any. (3) Unless excluded or modified (Section 2316) other implied warranties may arise from course of dealing or usage of trade. (Enacted b
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the warranty period and the exclusive remedy, significantly weakening the warranty provided to the distributor. This directly contradicts California Commercial Code § 2314, which establishes an implied warranty of merchantability, ensuring goods are fit for their ordinary purpose. The scraped law snippet 1 states, "Unless excluded or modified (Section 2316), a warranty that the goods shall be merchantable is implied in a contract for their sale if the seller is a merchant with respect to goods of that kind...Goods to be merchantable must be at least such as (c) Are fit for the ordinary purposes for which such goods are used." By omitting the specifics of the warranty and remedy, the modified text attempts to circumvent this implied warranty, creating a legal contradiction, as it allows STAAR to potentially avoid responsibility for non-merchantable products, violating consumer protection principles.

perturbation.original_text: 13.3 EXCEPT FOR EACH PARTY'S CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS SET FORTH IN SECTION 12 AND INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS SET FORTH IN THIS SECTION 13, WITHOUT LIMITING ANY RIGHT DISTRIBUTOR MAY HAVE UNDER LOCAL STATUTES THAT CANNOT BE EXCLUDED, RESTRICTED OR MODIFIED, NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE OR SPECIAL DAMAGES, OR FOR DAMAGES DUE TO LOSS OF PROFITS, LOSS OF BUSINESS, LOSS OF USE OR DATA, OR INTERRUPTION OF BUSINESS, EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.3 NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE OR SPECIAL DAMAGES, OR FOR DAMAGES DUE TO LOSS OF PROFITS, LOSS OF BUSINESS, LOSS OF USE OR DATA, OR INTERRUPTION OF BUSINESS, EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.
perturbation.explanation: The original text contains a clause preserving rights the distributor may have under local statutes that cannot be excluded, restricted, or modified. The modified text removes this clause, potentially leading to the distributor waiving rights provided under California law that are otherwise inalienable.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code § 1668 states that contracts that exempt anyone from responsibility for their own fraud, willful injury, or violation of law are against public policy. By removing the clause preserving the distributor's rights under local statutes that cannot be excluded, the contract might be interpreted as attempting to exempt STAAR from liability for such actions, potentially violating Cal. Civ. Code § 1668. For example, if STAAR engaged in fraudulent behavior, this modified clause could be interpreted as an attempt to limit its liability, which is prohibited under California law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1668 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> Code Search >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 4. UNLAWFUL CONTRACTS [1667 - 1670.12] ( Title 4 enacted 1872. ) 1668. All contracts which have for their object, directly or indirectly, to exempt any one from responsibility for his own fraud, or willful injury to the person or property of another, or violation of law, whether willful or negligent, are against the policy of the law. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes a clause preserving the distributor's rights under local statutes. According to the Law Explanation, this could be interpreted as an attempt to limit STAAR's liability for fraudulent behavior, violating California Civil Code § 1668. Scraped Law Snippet 1 explicitly states that contracts that exempt anyone from responsibility for their own fraud, willful injury, or violation of law are against public policy. Therefore, the omission in the changed text creates a legal contradiction by potentially removing protections afforded by law, aligning with the definition of 'Omission Legal Contradiction'.
